reasoning: The FRED series (CC4WSA) currently shows consistent short-term momentum upwards, resting at 1,868,500 on March 15, 2025. This has been driven partly by a reported spike in initial jobless claims in late February (jumping to 242,000), which will feed into continued claims throughout March and early April, strongly biasing near-term predictions toward an increase. The FRED tool's combined estimate erroneously used absolute 2024 levels vs 2025 thresholds to claim negative seasonality, but looking specifically at 2024 trends, claims typically hold or rise from mid-March to early April, and rise substantially through the summer. Therefore, short-term probabilities are >0.6. For the intermediate and distant horizons (6-12 months), the uncertainty increases materially, but there remains a slight upward longer-term structural trend in the data due to labor force growth and mild economic softening. Predictions smoothly decay toward ~0.5 as the forecast horizon expands in accordance with prompt instructions.
